title: PCIE2 Draft → Publish-Ready Queue Recovery
summary: Assembled 100 publish-ready rows from 200 PCIE2 drafts in one run. pcie2_publish_queue went from 0 → 100 ready. Publishing remains LOCKED (global stop + canary cap). Legacy publishers remain 410/blocked.

diagnosis.root_cause: PCIE2 creative drafts never assigned image_url or board_id; no component bridged drafts → pcie2_publish_queue.
diagnosis.fix: New canonical assembler pcie2-publish-assembler maps board from product slug/category, recovers image_url from product asset, builds canonical /products/{slug}?utm_source=pinterest destination, enforces banned-phrase + quality + idempotency.

idempotency: UNIQUE INDEX on pcie2_publish_queue(product_id, board_id, md5(image_url)) WHERE status IN (ready,queued,pending,publishing)
safety_gates: Assembler never calls Pinterest API; only writes pcie2_publish_queue

samples.headline: Safe Control for Your Dog's Post-Surgery Recovery Routine
Why Vets Recommend Safe Low-Level Rest After Their Move
Stop Letting Your Dog Lay on the Scorching Ground
9 out of 10 vets recommend soft play during recovery

high: Schedule the assembler hourly (cron) so new PCIE2 drafts auto-flow to queue.
Expand BANNED list to include 'vets recommend' & 'X out of N vets' style claims before Guardian re-evaluates queued rows.
medium: Add per-board fill quotas so no single board dominates the queue.
Add HEAD-check image validation in scheduled runs (skipped in recovery batch for speed).
low: Surface board_mapping_reason histogram in admin dashboard.
